data.title: Krona Public Real Estate (DB:927) One Off Gain Dominates FY 2025 Earnings Narratives
data.description:
Krona Public Real Estate (DB:927) has released its FY 2025 numbers with fourth quarter revenue of SEK22.4 million and basic EPS of SEK1.78, capping off a trailing twelve month period that produced revenue of SEK81.0 million and EPS of SEK6.93 alongside reported earnings growth of 26.8% over the year. The company has seen quarterly revenue range from SEK17.9 million to SEK22.4 million in recent periods, while quarterly EPS has moved between SEK0.99 and SEK2.09. This has resulted in a trailing net profit margin of 52.4% that sits next to a sizeable one off gain of SEK19.9 million in the past year. Against that backdrop, the latest results present a margin rich story that investors may review carefully given the role of one off items in the headline profitability.

See our full analysis for Krona Public Real Estate.

With the numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile aligns with the prevailing narratives around Krona Public Real Estate, and where those storylines might need a rethink.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

52.4% Margin Built On SEK19.9M One Off Gain

  • The trailing net profit margin stands at 52.4% on SEK81.0 million of revenue and SEK42.5 million of net income (excluding extra items), alongside a SEK19.9 million one off gain that is part of the reported picture.
  • What stands out for a bullish view is that earnings for the last year rose 26.8% while the company only recently became profitable over the past five years. However, the size of the SEK19.9 million one off gain means anyone leaning bullish needs to separate ongoing profit from that single event.
    • Bulls pointing to the 26.8% earnings growth can reference the SEK42.5 million of trailing net income, but they also have to account for how much of that ties back to the one off gain.
    • This tension between a 52.4% margin and the presence of SEK19.9 million in non recurring income is important if you are trying to judge how repeatable the current profitability really is.

Quarterly Net Income Swings From SEK5.6M To SEK17.0M

  • Within FY 2025, quarterly net income (excluding extra items) ranged from SEK5.6 million in Q3 2025 to SEK17.0 million in Q4 2025, alongside revenue between SEK19.3 million and SEK22.4 million and EPS between SEK0.99 and SEK1.78 over the same stretch.
  • Bears looking at these swings can argue that the pattern of net income moving from SEK10.5 million in Q1 2025 to SEK5.6 million in Q3 2025 and then to SEK17.0 million in Q4 2025 raises questions about how stable underlying earnings are from period to period.
    • Critics highlight that Q3 2025 net income of SEK5.6 million sits well below the SEK9.4 million to SEK10.5 million range seen in Q1 and Q2 2025, even though revenue stayed near SEK19 million in each of those quarters.
    • The rebound to SEK17.0 million of net income in Q4 2025 on SEK22.4 million of revenue gives bears a data point to watch, because it reinforces how much quarterly profit can move even when revenue changes are relatively modest.

P/E Of 8.5x Versus DCF Fair Value Of €10.24

  • The shares trade on a trailing P/E of 8.5x, which sits below the German real estate industry average of 10.7x and well below the peer average of 34x, while a DCF fair value of €10.24 is well above the current price of €3.02.
  • What is interesting for a more optimistic stance is that the low 8.5x P/E and the gap between the DCF fair value of €10.24 and the €3.02 share price appear alongside 26.8% earnings growth and a 52.4% net margin. Anyone arguing a bullish case has to weigh these valuation signals against the flagged financial risks of weak interest coverage and recent shareholder dilution.
    • Supporters of the bullish angle can point out that the combination of a P/E below both industry and peer averages and the higher DCF fair value all sit on a year when reported earnings rose 26.8%.
    • On the other hand, the same data set clearly states that interest payments are not well covered by earnings and shareholders were substantially diluted in the past year, which gives a counterweight to the apparent valuation gap.

Some investors see this mix of a low 8.5x P/E, a DCF fair value of €10.24, and identified financing risks as a story worth unpacking further through the community narratives for Krona Public Real Estate, which dig into how different investors interpret the same set of numbers. data.title: IAMGOLD (TSX:IMG) Margin Reset To 23.3% Tests Bullish Growth Narratives
data.description:
IAMGOLD (TSX:IMG) has wrapped up FY 2025 with Q4 revenue of US$1.1 billion and basic EPS of US$0.70, capping a year where the trailing twelve months show US$2.9 billion in revenue and EPS of US$1.16 as investors weigh how the latest numbers fit into the broader story. Over the past few quarters, the company has seen revenue move from US$477.1 million in Q1 2025 to US$706.7 million in Q3 and then US$1.1 billion in Q4. Quarterly EPS shifted from US$0.07 to US$0.24 and then US$0.70, which may prompt investors to focus closely on how margins are holding up across the gold portfolio.

See our full analysis for IAMGOLD.

With the headline figures on the table, the next step is to measure these results against the main narratives around IAMGOLD, examining where the numbers align with the story and where they may challenge it.

See what the community is saying about IAMGOLD

Advertisement

Margins Reset To 23.3% On Trailing Basis

  • On the trailing 12 month view, IAMGOLD reported net profit of US$664.4 million on US$2.9 billion of revenue, which works out to a 23.3% net margin compared with 50.2% in the prior year data point.
  • Consensus narrative points to higher costs at Côté and Essakane as a key concern, and the margin move in the data lines up with that caution:
    • Net income on a trailing basis is US$664.4 million versus US$819.6 million in the prior year data, even though trailing revenue is higher at US$2.9 billion versus US$1.6 billion. That is consistent with margin compression rather than volume weakness.
    • Analysts are also assuming margin compression ahead, from 40.6% to 22.2% over the next three years in their balanced view. This sits very close to the current 23.3% trailing margin and suggests they are treating this lower margin level as a realistic ongoing reference point.

EPS Trend And Growth Forecasts Pull In Different Directions

  • Within FY 2025, basic EPS moved from US$0.07 in Q1 to US$0.14 in Q2, US$0.24 in Q3 and US$0.70 in Q4, while the trailing 12 month EPS at Q4 2025 is US$1.16 compared with US$1.52 at Q4 2024.
  • Bulls highlight forecast EPS growth of about 16.45% a year, and the reported numbers give them some support but also a reality check:
    • The step up in quarterly EPS through FY 2025, from US$0.07 to US$0.70, fits the bullish story that ramp up at Côté and improving production can lift earnings, especially with trailing gold output in the data rising from 20.7 troy ounces to 23.8 troy ounces.
    • At the same time, trailing EPS has moved from US$1.52 to US$1.16, so anyone leaning on the bullish view that earnings can reach US$843.8 million by 2028 needs to reconcile those ambitions with a recent year where total earnings in the dataset are lower than the prior trailing period.
If you want to see how optimistic investors think this earnings path could get, check out the detailed bull case for IAMGOLD here 🐂 IAMGOLD Bull Case

Valuation Gap Versus DCF And Analyst Target

  • With the share price at CA$29.17, IAMGOLD is shown trading below a DCF fair value of CA$47.57 and below an analyst price target of CA$35.62, while its P/E of 18.9x sits under both the 69.1x peer average and the 23.7x Canadian Metals & Mining average.
  • Bears point to margin pressure and a weaker recent profit year, and the numbers partly back that caution even though the valuation data looks supportive:
    • The shift in trailing net margin from 50.2% to 23.3% and the commentary that earnings were negative in the most recent year relative to a strong five year trend show why some investors worry that today’s earnings base might not fully match the long term track record implied by the valuation gap.
    • On the other hand, the combination of a 38.7% discount to the DCF fair value, a lower P/E than both peers and the broader industry, and forecasts for revenue growth of about 6.3% a year puts clear numerical weight behind the idea that the market is already pricing in a fair amount of those bearish concerns.
Skeptics are focused on those thinner margins, so if you want the other side of the story, the full bear case on IAMGOLD is worth a look 🐻 IAMGOLD Bear Case

Next Steps

To see how these results tie into long-term growth, risks, and valuation, check out the full range of community narratives for IAMGOLD on Simply Wall St. Add the company to your watchlist or portfolio so you'll be alerted when the story evolves.

With sentiment clearly split between the bullish and bearish readings of these results, it makes sense to review the data yourself and move quickly to your own view. data.title: NVIDIA Meta And India Deals Shape Global AI Infrastructure Story
data.description:
• NVIDIA (NasdaqGS:NVDA) agreed a multi year, multigenerational partnership with Meta to supply millions of AI chips, along with GPUs, CPUs, networking and privacy technologies for use across Meta’s hyperscale data centers.
  • The company also announced a US$2b deployment with Yotta Data Services, involving 20,736 Blackwell Ultra GPUs to build what is described as Asia’s largest AI supercluster in India.
  • These agreements expand NVIDIA’s role in global AI infrastructure and position India as an emerging hub for high performance AI compute.

NVIDIA sits at the center of the data center and AI compute stack, selling chips and full platform solutions that power training and inference workloads. Partnerships with hyperscalers such as Meta and regional providers such as Yotta illustrate how demand for large scale AI clusters is spreading across both US and international markets. For investors, this highlights that AI infrastructure is being built out across multiple geographies and customer types, rather than being concentrated solely among a few US cloud providers.

Looking ahead, readers may want to watch how NVIDIA executes on these long duration supply commitments and how quickly partners ramp actual deployments. The scale of the Yotta build in India also brings attention to local regulatory, power, and data sovereignty considerations that may influence how efficiently these AI clusters are utilized.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for NVIDIA by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on NVIDIA.

2 things going right for NVIDIA that this headline doesn't cover.

For NVIDIA, the Meta and Yotta deals speak to two different but connected angles of the AI compute story. Meta’s commitment to use millions of current and future NVIDIA chips, plus Grace CPUs and networking, reinforces NVIDIA’s role as a full-stack supplier at hyperscale. That is important when rivals like AMD, Intel and in-house accelerators are all competing for data center sockets. The Yotta deployment in India shows how demand for high performance AI infrastructure is spreading into new regions, with over US$2b of Blackwell Ultra hardware plus a multi year DGX Cloud engagement anchoring NVIDIA inside an emerging AI hub rather than just selling chips into existing US data centers.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The NVIDIA Narrative

  • The long duration commitments from Meta and Yotta line up with the narrative that AI infrastructure buildouts underpin multi year demand visibility for NVIDIA’s platforms and software.
  • Concentration in a few very large customers, such as Meta, also echoes narrative concerns that hyperscalers developing custom silicon could eventually pressure NVIDIA’s pricing power or share if those efforts ramp.
  • The scale up in India through Yotta and other local partners adds a regional AI infrastructure angle that is only partly reflected in the existing narrative, which is more focused on US hyperscalers and sovereign projects elsewhere.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

data.title: Companies Like Grace Therapeutics (NASDAQ:GRCE) Are In A Position To Invest In Growth
data.description:
Just because a business does not make any money, does not mean that the stock will go down. For example, although software-as-a-service business Salesforce.com lost money for years while it grew recurring revenue, if you held shares since 2005, you'd have done very well indeed. But the harsh reality is that very many loss making companies burn through all their cash and go bankrupt.

So should Grace Therapeutics (NASDAQ:GRCE) shareholders be worried about its cash burn? For the purposes of this article, cash burn is the annual rate at which an unprofitable company spends cash to fund its growth; its negative free cash flow. We'll start by comparing its cash burn with its cash reserves in order to calculate its cash runway.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

Does Grace Therapeutics Have A Long Cash Runway?

You can calculate a company's cash runway by dividing the amount of cash it has by the rate at which it is spending that cash. When Grace Therapeutics last reported its December 2025 balance sheet in February 2026, it had zero debt and cash worth US$19m. In the last year, its cash burn was US$10m. That means it had a cash runway of around 22 months as of December 2025. That's not too bad, but it's fair to say the end of the cash runway is in sight, unless cash burn reduces drastically. You can see how its cash balance has changed over time in the image below.

See our latest analysis for Grace Therapeutics

How Is Grace Therapeutics' Cash Burn Changing Over Time?

Grace Therapeutics didn't record any revenue over the last year, indicating that it's an early stage company still developing its business. Nonetheless, we can still examine its cash burn trajectory as part of our assessment of its cash burn situation. While it hardly paints a picture of imminent growth, the fact that it has reduced its cash burn by 28% over the last year suggests some degree of prudence. While the past is always worth studying, it is the future that matters most of all. For that reason, it makes a lot of sense to take a look at our analyst forecasts for the company.

How Easily Can Grace Therapeutics Raise Cash?

While Grace Therapeutics is showing a solid reduction in its cash burn, it's still worth considering how easily it could raise more cash, even just to fuel faster growth. Issuing new shares, or taking on debt, are the most common ways for a listed company to raise more money for its business. One of the main advantages held by publicly listed companies is that they can sell shares to investors to raise cash and fund growth. By looking at a company's cash burn relative to its market capitalisation, we gain insight on how much shareholders would be diluted if the company needed to raise enough cash to cover another year's cash burn.

Since it has a market capitalisation of US$61m, Grace Therapeutics' US$10m in cash burn equates to about 17% of its market value. As a result, we'd venture that the company could raise more cash for growth without much trouble, albeit at the cost of some dilution.

Is Grace Therapeutics' Cash Burn A Worry?

The good news is that in our view Grace Therapeutics' cash burn situation gives shareholders real reason for optimism. Not only was its cash burn reduction quite good, but its cash runway was a real positive. While we're the kind of investors who are always a bit concerned about the risks involved with cash burning companies, the metrics we have discussed in this article leave us relatively comfortable about Grace Therapeutics' situation. On another note, we conducted an in-depth investigation of the company, and identified 5 warning signs for Grace Therapeutics (2 are concerning!) that you should be aware of before investing here.

data.title: Be Sure To Check Out United Fire Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:UFCS) Before It Goes Ex-Dividend
data.description:
United Fire Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:UFCS) stock is about to trade ex-dividend in 4 days. The ex-dividend date occurs one day before the record date, which is the day on which shareholders need to be on the company's books in order to receive a dividend. It is important to be aware of the ex-dividend date because any trade on the stock needs to have been settled on or before the record date. Therefore, if you purchase United Fire Group's shares on or after the 24th of February, you won't be eligible to receive the dividend, when it is paid on the 10th of March.

The company's next dividend payment will be US$0.20 per share, on the back of last year when the company paid a total of US$0.64 to shareholders. Last year's total dividend payments show that United Fire Group has a trailing yield of 2.1% on the current share price of US$38.66. Dividends are a major contributor to investment returns for long term holders, but only if the dividend continues to be paid. That's why we should always check whether the dividend payments appear sustainable, and if the company is growing.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Dividends are typically paid out of company income, so if a company pays out more than it earned, its dividend is usually at a higher risk of being cut. United Fire Group is paying out just 14% of its profit after tax, which is comfortably low and leaves plenty of breathing room in the case of adverse events.

Companies that pay out less in dividends than they earn in profits generally have more sustainable dividends. The lower the payout ratio, the more wiggle room the business has before it could be forced to cut the dividend.

See our latest analysis for United Fire Group

Click here to see how much of its profit United Fire Group paid out over the last 12 months.

Advertisement

Have Earnings And Dividends Been Growing?

Stocks in companies that generate sustainable earnings growth often make the best dividend prospects, as it is easier to lift the dividend when earnings are rising. If earnings decline and the company is forced to cut its dividend, investors could watch the value of their investment go up in smoke. It's encouraging to see United Fire Group has grown its earnings rapidly, up 37% a year for the past five years.

Another key way to measure a company's dividend prospects is by measuring its historical rate of dividend growth. It looks like the United Fire Group dividends are largely the same as they were 10 years ago.

To Sum It Up

Has United Fire Group got what it takes to maintain its dividend payments? Typically, companies that are growing rapidly and paying out a low fraction of earnings are keeping the profits for reinvestment in the business. This strategy can add significant value to shareholders over the long term - as long as it's done without issuing too many new shares. We think this is a pretty attractive combination, and would be interested in investigating United Fire Group more closely.

While it's tempting to invest in United Fire Group for the dividends alone, you should always be mindful of the risks involved. To help with this, we've discovered 1 warning sign for United Fire Group that you should be aware of before investing in their shares.
